channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: AFP highlights need for careful planning in Middle East rescue operations for OFWs
item.description: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) emphasized the importance of thorough and strategic planning for rescue missions involving Overseas Filipino Workers (OFWs) in the Middle East. According to A...

item.title: Canadian Conservatives Advocate for Easier Access to Nicotine Pouches
item.description: Nicotine pouches, such as Zyn, are gaining attention among conservative politicians in Canada. While Zyn itself is not approved for sale in the country, its competitor Zonnic is available behind pharm...

item.title: Lok Sabha Debates No-Confidence Motion Against Speaker Om Birla Amidst Objections on Chairmanship
item.description: On Tuesday, the Lok Sabha witnessed a heated debate over a no-confidence motion seeking the removal of Speaker Om Birla. The motion was read out by Congress MP Mohammad Jawed and seconded by more than...

item.title: Identifying and Managing Dark Personality Traits in Work and Personal Life
item.description: Psychologist Leanne ten Brinke has devoted decades to studying individuals with dark personality traits, including psychopathy, narcissism, Machiavellianism, and sadism. Her research indicates that th...

item.title: Federal High Court sets new trial dates for man accused of inciting military overthrow on social media
item.description: The Federal High Court for Abuja don fix new dates, 18 and 19 May, for trial of Innocent Chukwuma, wey dem accuse say e use him X (formerly Twitter) account to call make military remove President Bola...

item.title: Promoting responsible pet ownership in South Africa
item.description: Responsible pet parenting is gaining attention across South Africa as more pet owners recognise the importance of proper care and attention for their furry companions. Dotsure, a local pet insurance p...

item.title: Five Iranian women’s footballers granted protection in Australia
item.description: Early on Tuesday morning, five members of the Iranian women’s football team were officially granted protection in Australia by Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke, following several days of intense specu...

item.title: Glasgow Central Station Closed Following Nearby Blaze, Local Businesses Affected
item.description: Glasgow Central, Scotland's busiest railway station, remains closed for a third consecutive day following a significant fire at a nearby vape shop on Union Street. The blaze, which began on Sunday aft...

item.title: Cheltenham Festival Day One: JP McManus Targets Multiple Wins and Sir Anthony McCoy Prepares for Marathon
item.description: The opening day of the 2026 Cheltenham Festival promises excitement and strong competition, with notable figures in horse racing taking centre stage. Legendary owner JP McManus is poised for success, ...

item.title: Employment fraud threatens Kenyan public sector pensions
item.description: In Kenya, a serious pension crisis is unfolding, driven by widespread employment fraud, corruption, and administrative weaknesses. Investigations show that hundreds of public sector employees obtained...

item.title: Lessons from Iraq: Risks of Military Action Without Political Planning in Iran
item.description: The article analyzes the aftermath of the 2003 U.S. invasion of Iraq and draws parallels with the recent U.S.-Israeli military strikes on Iran. While the U.S. achieved its military objectives in Iraq—...

item.title: Bengaluru Hotels and Restaurants Face Closure Risk Due to LPG Supply Halt
item.description: Restaurants and hotels in Bengaluru are facing a potential crisis due to a sudden halt in the supply of commercial LPG cylinders. Industry representatives have reported that the shortage is linked to ...

item.title: Sindh announces two-week school closure and Friday WFH for govt offices under austerity plan
item.description: The Sindh government has declared a set of austerity measures aimed at mitigating the economic impact of ongoing regional tensions. Schools in the province will observe a spring vacation from March 16...

item.title: Boniface Mwangi Advocates Harsh Measures Against Corruption and Outlines Governance Reforms
item.description: Nairobi — Activist and presidential aspirant Boniface Mwangi has sparked strong debate after proposing that corrupt public officials be hanged in public if convicted. Speaking on Capital in the Mornin...

item.title: LHRC warns over children being held in serious criminal case at Bunda Court
item.description: The Legal and Human Rights Centre (LHRC) in Tanzania has expressed strong concern over two minors involved in a serious criminal case at Bunda District Court. According to LHRC, the two children, iden...

item.title: ANC Sedibeng outlines strategy to stabilise municipalities and improve service delivery
item.description: The ANC in the Sedibeng region has announced that stabilising municipalities and improving service delivery will be its main focus ahead of this year's local government elections. Regional Secretary J...

item.title: King Wanda and The Don exit Big Brother Mzansi after reaching top 10
item.description: King Wanda, a 31-year-old municipal employee from Delmas, and The Don have officially exited the Big Brother Mzansi Bazozwa house following the live eviction show on Sunday. King Wanda expressed pride...

item.title: Pakistan Concludes 5G Spectrum Auction: 480 MHz Sold for $507 Million to Zong, Jazz and Ufone
item.description: The Government of Pakistan successfully completed its long-awaited 5G spectrum auction in Islamabad on March 10, 2026. A total of 480 megahertz (MHz) of spectrum was sold for $507 million after three ...

item.title: Lulu-Briggs Appreciates Tinubu’s Support for State Police and Restored Peace in Rivers
item.description: Chief Dumu Lulu-Briggs, former Accord Party governorship candidate in Rivers State, don commend President Bola Ahmed Tinibu for supporting State Police in Nigeria. For one-on-one chat with journalists...

item.title: Defence Argues Abbotsford Double Homicide Suspect May Not Be Guilty of Murder
item.description: Closing arguments continued in the trial of three men accused of the 2022 double homicide of Abbotsford residents Arnold and Joanne De Jong. The defence lawyer for Abhijeet Singh argued that while his...
